exports.content: Adding 3.8-5.7 mol% MDACl2 to FAPbI3 stabilizes the alpha-phase against transition to the delta-phase even after 24 hours at 80% humidity, whereas pure FAPbI3 and FAPbI3 with only 1.9 mol% MDACl2 completely convert to the delta-phase
exports.label: alpha_phase_stabilization

exports.content: FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 films exhibit nonradiative recombination lifetime of 1562 ns on quartz, more than double the control value of 715 ns, indicating reduced trap-assisted recombination
exports.label: carrier_lifetime_1562_ns

exports.content: FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 PSC achieves certified stabilized PCE of 23.73% via quasi-steady-state method at Newport, with Jsc=26.1 mA/cm2, Voc=1.15 V, FF=79.0%, the highest reported for mp-TiO2-based PSCs
exports.label: certified_23_7_pct

exports.content: XPS and TOF-SIMS show higher residual Cl- content in FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 than in the MAPbBr3 control, concentrated at the TiO2/perovskite interface, which is expected to enhance light stability of the PSC
exports.label: cl_residue_at_interface

exports.content: Space-charge-limited current measurements show electron trap densities of 5.7x10^15 cm-3 for FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2, lower than 1.0x10^16 cm-3 for the MAPbBr3 control, indicating that FA vacancy defects do not act as deep electron traps
exports.label: defect_density_low

exports.content: FAPbI3 perovskite solar cells on mesoporous TiO2/FTO substrates using anti-solvent method with methylenediammonium dichloride (MDACl2) added to the FAPbI3 precursor at 1.9-5.7 mol%, with CuPC (copper phthalocyanine) or spiro-OMeTAD as hole transport material, Au electrode, measured under AM 1.5G at 100 mW/cm2
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: Unencapsulated target device retains >90% of initial PCE after 70 hours at 85% RH and 25C, while the MAPbBr3-stabilized control degrades to 40% of initial PCE
exports.label: humidity_stability_85rh

exports.content: MDACl2 addition preserves the inherent narrow bandgap of FAPbI3: PL emission shifts minimally from 826 nm (pristine) to 822 nm (3.8 mol%), compared to 816 nm for the MAPbBr3-stabilized control, enabling higher photocurrent through broader absorption
exports.label: inherent_bandgap_preserved

exports.content: Can MDACl2-stabilized FAPbI3 devices maintain >90% PCE beyond 1000 hours of continuous operation under damp-heat conditions (85C/85% RH), which is the industry standard IEC 61215 test?
exports.label: long_term_operational_stability

exports.content: Methylenediammonium dichloride (MDACl2) is a divalent diammonium dopant with ionic radius (262 pm) comparable to FA+ (256 pm); MDA2+ can form more hydrogen bonds with I- than FA+ or MA+ due to having more H atoms, potentially stabilizing the alpha-FAPbI3 phase in smaller amounts than MA
exports.label: mdac12_dopant

exports.content: MDA2+ stabilizes alpha-FAPbI3 through two mechanisms: (1) stronger hydrogen bonding with I- due to more H atoms than FA+ or MA+, and (2) stronger ionic interaction from its divalent charge state, enabling stabilization in smaller amounts than MA
exports.label: mida_hbond_mechanism

exports.content: What is the optimal MDACl2 concentration window for balancing alpha-phase stability against defect formation from FA vacancies, and does the MDACl2 approach transfer to mixed-halide or mixed-cation perovskite compositions?
exports.label: optimal_mda_concentration

exports.content: Encapsulated target device with spiro-OMeTAD HTM maintains ~90% of initial PCE (>23.0%) over 600 hours of maximum power point tracking under full solar illumination (AM 1.5G, 100 mW/cm2) in ambient conditions without UV filter
exports.label: photostability_600h_mppt

exports.content: A second certified device achieves Jsc=26.70 mA/cm2 (highest reported for FAPbI3-based PSCs) with Voc=1.144 V and FF=77.56%, corresponding to stabilized PCE of 23.69%
exports.label: record_jsc_26_7

exports.content: Unencapsulated target device retains >90% of initial PCE after 20 hours annealing at 150C in air (~25% RH), while the control degrades to <20% of initial PCE due to MA evaporation from the MAPbBr3 component
exports.label: thermal_stability_150c

exports.content: The best-performing lab-measured device achieves PCE of 24.66% under reverse scan with Jsc=26.50 mA/cm2, Voc=1.14 V, and FF=81.77%
exports.label: uncertified_24_66_pct
